violations.text: The door to the kitchen toilet room is not self-closing. Toilet room doors that open to a food preparation space shall be self-closing to limit potential contamination. New Violation. Correct By: 02/07/2026

The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was blocked by a stool and green scrubbing pads hung on the faucet. A hand-washing sink shall be accessible at all times for employee use. Stool and green scrubbing pads were moved during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/02/2024

Fried eggs are served raw or undercooked to the customer's request. A written consumer advisory (on the menu, table tent, or placard) is not provided to the consumer informing them of the significantly increased risk associated with certain vulnerable consumers eating such food in a raw or undercooked form. PIC added consumer advisory to menu board during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/02/2024

Proper cooling methods were not used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods. Tomato jam that was recently prepared and cooling on stove was observed being covered by PIC prior to the food item reaching an internal temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below. Tomato jam was observed at 95 degrees Fahrenheit. Proper cooling methods were discussed during time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/02/2024
The prep table surface next to the stove cooktop was observed with foil covering the shelf surface and were not in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 10/09/2024

The cutting boards in the kitchen and yogurt prep area are very scratched and scored. Cutting blocks and boards that are subject to scratching and scoring shall be resurfaced if they can no longer be effectively cleaned and sanitized, or discarded if they are not capable of being resurfaced. New Violation. Correct By: 10/16/2024

The non-food contact surfaces of the refrigerator door gaskets and doors have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-food contact surfaces shall be cleaned at a frequency necessary to prevent the accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 10/02/2024
